reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: In the immediate previous game (Game 6), I successfully volunteered in Round 1 using the initial, unverified capsules from all players. This resulted in a collective escape with maximum coins for everyone. This outcome strongly indicates that, at least in certain game configurations, the initial answers provided by each player are correct. To maximize my coins, ensure everyone's escape, and avoid the high risk of random elimination (which has targeted me in prior rounds when no one volunteered), the most effective strategy is to replicate the successful approach from Game 6 and volunteer immediately with the initial capsules. I am confident in my own answer for Q1, and based on Game 6, I have a high degree of confidence in the others' initial capsules as well.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: It is Round 1 and no one has verified any answers yet. Volunteering now would be extremely risky as I only know my own answer for Q2. Passing carries a 25% risk of random death. Verifying another player's answer is the best way to gather information, build consensus, and reduce the overall risk for the group. I will verify Q1 to start contributing to the puzzle-solving effort.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: In the first round, there is no consensus on any of the answers, making it far too risky to volunteer. Passing also carries a significant risk of random elimination (25% chance) and doesn't advance the game. Verifying an answer is the most strategic move to gather information and start building consensus. I will verify Q1, which currently only has Player A's input. This will either confirm Player A's answer (building initial consensus) or reveal a conflict, both of which provide valuable information for the group to progress towards a safe escape. This costs 1 coin, leaving me with 3. I will not verify my own answer (Q3) as that provides no new information from other players.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
